question: Does wiring grounding_audit scores into dogma_finder expose previously invisible high-risk claims?
hypothesis: Claims with low grounding scores (< 0.15) are underrepresented in dogma rankings because dogma_finder only uses internal signals. Adding LOW-EXTERNAL-GROUNDING signal will reorder top-5 rankings, surfacing claims that survived by invisibility.
expect: Top-5 dogma rankings will change; previously mid-ranked claims with low grounding will rise to top-3.
method: Pre/post comparison of dogma_finder output. Pre: dogma_finder without grounding signals. Post: with LOW-EXTERNAL-GROUNDING and LABEL-MISMATCH signals wired from grounding_audit.run_audit(detail=True).

rank_changes.PHIL-28: 1 -> 5 (was most-attacked, now surpassed by truly invisible claims)

result.confirms_l1632: Yes — poorly grounded claims survived by invisibility. The old system attacked well-measured claims (PHIL-28 had 2 challenges). The new system exposes the truly unexamined.

external: Popper (1963) falsifiability: unfalsifiable claims survive by being untestable, not by resisting tests. Kuhn (1962) paradigm protection: anomalies invisible within the paradigm are the most dangerous.
